segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: species, is the sign of a highly successful tree-planting scheme.
segments.zh: 物种是植树计划非常成功的标志。为了确保人工林的生存和恢复力，使用具有适当遗传多样性水平的树种至关重要：遗传多样性是物种内发现的遗传变异量，对它们的生存至关重要。使用遗传多样性低的种子通常会降低恢复森林的恢复力，这会使它们容易受到疾病的影响，并且无法适应气候变化。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: To ensure the survival and resilience of a planted forest, it's vital to use tree seeds with appropriate levels of genetic diversity: the amount of genetic variation found within a species essential for their survival.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Using seeds with low genetic diversity generally lowers the resilience of restored forests, which can make them vulnerable to disease and unable to adapt to climate change.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Choosing the right location for reforestation projects is as important as choosing the right trees.
segments.zh: 为再造林项目选择合适的地点与选择合适的树木同样重要。最终，最好的植树区域是那些以前有森林但状况不佳的地方。最好避免非森林景观，如天然草原、稀树草原或湿地，因为这些生态系统已经对碳捕获做出了很大贡献。选择树木可以提供其他好处的区域也是有利的，例如休闲空间。应避免在目前用于农业的区域进行再造林，因为这通常会导致其他地区的森林砍伐。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Ultimately, the best area for planting trees would be in formerly forested areas that are in poor condition.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: It's better to avoid non-forested landscapes such as natural grasslands, savannas or wetlands as these ecosystems already contribute greatly to capturing carbon.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: It would also be advantageous to choose an area where trees could provide other benefits, such as recreational spaces.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Reforesting areas which are currently exploited for agriculture should be avoided as this often leads to other areas being deforested.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Large-scale reforestation projects require careful planning.
segments.zh: 大规模的再造林项目需要仔细规划。关于在哪里植树做出正确的决定取决于拥有正确的信息。拥有详细且最新的地图来识别需要干预的高优先级区域至关重要。无人机技术是帮助优先考虑和监测退化森林区域进行恢复的有用工具。在巴西，它被用来识别和量化亚马逊部分地区因人类活动（如养牛和非法伐木）而遭受破坏的情况。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Making the right decisions about where to plant trees depends on having the right information.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Having detailed and up-to-date maps identifying high-priority areas for intervention is essential.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Drone technology is a useful tool in helping to prioritise and monitor areas of degraded forest for restoration.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: In Brazil, it's being used to identify and quantify how parts of the Amazon are being devastated by human activities such as rearing cattle and illegal logging.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: A good example of where the right trees were picked to achieve a restored forest is in Lampang Province in Northern Thailand.
segments.zh: 一个选择正确树木以实现森林恢复的好例子是泰国北部的南邦府。一个以前有森林但因采矿而退化的地点由一家水泥公司与清迈大学一起进行了再造林。在铺设了60厘米的表土后，他们种植了14种不同的本地树种，其中包括几种榕树。榕树是一个关键物种，因为它们在维持野生动物种群方面发挥着关键作用。它们是热带再造林项目的核心，因为它们通过吸引作为天然种子传播者的动物和鸟类来加速恢复过程的速度。这有助于通过多种植物物种的健康再生来促进多样性。与大多数果树不同，榕树全年结果，为许多物种提供可靠的食物来源。例如，在这个地点，仅仅三个雨季之后，猴子就开始来吃榕树果实，通过排便自然传播种子。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: A previously forested site which had been degraded through mining was reforested by a cement company together with Chiang Mai University.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: After spreading 60 cm of topsoil, they planted 14 different native tree species which included several species of fig.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Figs are a keystone species because of the critical role they play in maintaining wildlife populations.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: They are central to tropical reforestation projects as they accelerate the speed of the recovery process by attracting animals and birds which act as natural seed dispersers.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: This helps to promote diversity through the healthy regrowth of a wide range of plant species.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Unlike the majority of fruit trees, figs bear fruit all year round, providing a reliable food source for many species.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: At this site, for example, after only three rainy seasons, monkeys started visiting to eat the fig fruits, naturally dispersing seeds through defecation.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Reforestation projects should always aim to make sure that local communities are consulted and involved in the decision-making process.
segments.zh: 再造林项目应始终确保当地社区被咨询并参与决策过程。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: The restoration of mangrove forests in Madagascar is an example of a project which has succeeded in creating real benefits for the community.
segments.zh: 马达加斯加红树林的恢复是一个成功为社区创造真正利益的项目例子。红树林的破坏对植物和动物生命产生了可怕的影响，也严重影响了渔业，而渔业是沿海地区当地居民的主要就业来源。再造林项目涉及雇佣当地人来种植和照料新的红树林。现在已经种植了数百万棵红树林，这导致了一个健康的水生生态系统的回归。红树林还起到了防御气候变化导致的洪水威胁增加的作用。更重要的是，当地经济更加稳定，成千上万的马达加斯加人现在能够送孩子上学。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Destruction of the mangrove forests had a terrible impact on plant and animal life, and also badly affected the fishing industry, which was a major source of employment for local people living in coastal areas.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: The reforestation project involved hiring local people to plant and care for the new mangrove trees.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Millions of mangrove trees have now been planted which has resulted in the return of a healthy aquatic ecosystem.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: The mangroves also act as a defence against the increased threat of flooding caused by climate change.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: What's more, the local economy is more stable and thousands more Madagascans are now able to send their children to school.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: One other important point to consider . . .
segments.zh: 另一个需要考虑的重要点是……

items.prompt: Reforestation projects should: not include invasive species because of possible 31 ________ with native species
Reforestation projects should: aim to capture carbon, protect the environment and provide sustainable sources of 32 ________ for local people
Reforestation projects should: use tree seeds with a high genetic diversity to increase resistance to 33 ________ and climate change

pairs.note: 原文说'vulnerable to'（易受...影响），题干说'resistance to'（抵抗），是反义替换。
原文和题干都出现了'disease'，是原词重现。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句提到'vulnerable to disease and unable to adapt to climate change'，题干问'resistance to ___ and climate change'，通过反义替换和原词重现找到答案'disease'。

items.prompt: Reforestation projects should: plant trees on previously forested land which is in a bad condition, not select land which is being used for 34 ________

pairs.note: being used for 被替换为 exploited for，意为'被用于'，是常见同义替换。
题干中的 agriculture 在原文中直接出现，是原词复现。

paraphrase.explain: 题干问的是不应选择被用于什么用途的土地，定位句提到 'areas which are currently exploited for agriculture should be avoided'，其中 exploited for 对应 being used for，agriculture 原词出现，因此答案为 agriculture。

items.prompt: Large-scale reforestation projects: Base planning decisions on information from accurate 35 ________

pairs.note: accurate 被替换为 detailed and up-to-date，表示精确的、最新的信息。
原文中 maps 就是信息的具体形式，直接对应。

paraphrase.explain: 题干问的是基于精确的什么信息做决策，定位句提到需要 detailed and up-to-date maps，因此 maps 是答案。

items.prompt: Large-scale reforestation projects: Drones are useful for identifying areas in Brazil which are endangered by keeping 36 ________ and illegal logging.

pairs.note: keeping 和 rearing 都表示'饲养'，原文用 rearing cattle 表示养牛。

paraphrase.explain: 题干问 drones 在巴西用于识别哪些活动威胁区域，定位句提到'rearing cattle and illegal logging'，其中'rearing cattle'对应题干'keeping cattle'，所以答案填 cattle。

items.prompt: Lampang Province, Northern Thailand: increasing the 37 ________ of recovery by attracting animals and birds

pairs.p: accelerate the speed of the recovery process
pairs.note: 题干用'increasing'替换原文'加速'，但核心是'speed'被直接保留。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句提到'accelerate the speed of the recovery process'，其中'speed'与题干'speed'完全对应，且'accelerate'与'increasing'同义，因此答案就是'speed'。

items.prompt: Lampang Province, Northern Thailand: e.g., 38 ________ were soon attracted to the area.

pairs.q: 38 ________ were soon attracted to the area
pairs.p: monkeys started visiting to eat the fig fruits
pairs.note: 原文直接出现monkeys，与答案一致，是原词重现。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句提到'monkeys started visiting to eat the fig fruits'，其中'monkeys'就是题干中要填的38空，属于原词重现，直接定位。

items.prompt: Involving local communities: Destruction of mangrove forests in Madagascar made it difficult for people to make a living from 39 ________

pairs.note: 'make a living from' 对应 'source of employment'，都表示谋生手段。
题干中的 'fishing' 在原文中直接以 'fishing industry' 出现，是核心答案。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句提到 'badly affected the fishing industry, which was a major source of employment'，与题干 'make a living from fishing' 对应，因此答案就是 fishing。

items.prompt: Involving local communities: protects against the higher risk of 40 ________

pairs.q: protects against the higher risk of
pairs.p: act as a defence against the increased threat of
pairs.note: 题干用'protects against'替换原文'act as a defence against'，'higher risk'替换'increased threat'。
答案词在原文中直接出现。

paraphrase.explain: 定位到原文'act as a defence against the increased threat of flooding'，其中'act as a defence against'对应题干'protects against'，'increased threat'对应'higher risk'，答案词'flooding'直接出现。
